user_query:
Set up a civilian threat-monitoring and briefing plan for Dubai using the references provided. Read all three files under input/ (input/config-reference.md, input/country-profile-uae.json, input/seed_scan_log.json). Then produce the following artifacts under output/:

1) output/aegis-config.json — a valid minimal working configuration that meets all of these constraints exactly:
- location.country = "AE", location.city = "Dubai", location.timezone = "Asia/Dubai"
- language = "en"
- alerts.critical_instant = true
- briefings.enabled = true, briefings.morning = "08:00", briefings.evening = "20:00"
- scan_interval_minutes = 15
- llm.enabled = false (provider should be "none" or omit provider entirely)
- filters.require_location_match = true
- tone = "factual"
- include_preparedness = true
- api_keys.newsapi = null (do not include any API keys)
- sources.disabled may be an empty array (no custom sources required)

2) Two situation briefings that civilians can act on, using the provided Situation Update format below and informed by input/seed_scan_log.json and input/country-profile-uae.json:
- output/briefings/morning.md
- output/briefings/evening.md
Each briefing must include these sections/lines, in English:
- A header containing the exact phrase "SITUATION UPDATE — Dubai, UAE"
- A "Status:" line that includes one of these labels: CRITICAL, HIGH, or MEDIUM
- A section titled "What you should do:" with 4–6 concrete action bullets (use "- " or "→ " at the start of each bullet)
- A section titled "How this affects daily life:" that includes lines starting with each of: "Flights:", "Schools:", "Work:", "Supplies:", "Roads:", "Hospitals:"
- A section or line including the word "Outlook"
- A section or line including the word "Sources"
- An emergency line containing "Emergency: 999" and a reference to NCEMA
- A short disclaimer that tells readers to follow official government guidance (include the words "official" and "guidance")
Keep the tone calm, factual, and action-oriented. Do not speculate beyond the seed scan log; avoid panic language.

3) output/ops/operations.md — an operations note that explains the safety and integrity approach in plain English, covering all of:
- Anti-Hoax Protocol
- Source Tiers
- CRITICAL Validation (mention regex/negative patterns, that semantic checks are disabled, and include the term "fail-open")
- Corroboration (explicitly use the word "corroboration")
- Posting Schedule (briefings vs. critical alerts) and cooldowns

4) output/changelog.jsonl — append exactly one JSON line documenting the config creation, with this shape:
{"event":"config_created","timestamp":"<ISO8601>","rationale":"<short reason>"}

Situation Update format (for the two .md files):
SITUATION UPDATE — Dubai, UAE
<date and local time optional>
Status: <CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM> — short descriptor

Summary — 2–4 sentences in plain English with real numbers if available

What you should do:
- <action 1>
- <action 2>
- <action 3>
- <action 4>
(5th and 6th action optional)

How this affects daily life:
Flights: ...
Schools: ...
Work: ...
Supplies: ...
Roads: ...
Hospitals: ...

Outlook — next 12–24 hours
Sources — where to verify and get more information
Emergency: 999 | NCEMA | Follow official guidance
